channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Thika High School Student Dies in Prayer-Related Fight
item.description: A Grade 10 student died at Thika High School on July 11, sparking nationwide grief in Kenya. The principal, Ndungu Nyoro, shared details of the incident during an interview with Kiambu News, describin...

item.title: Dubai and Tanzania Strengthen Tourism Collaboration for Mutual Growth
item.description: Dubai is intensifying efforts to enhance its tourism partnership with Tanzania, aiming to boost visitor numbers through collaboration with local travel industry stakeholders. During a recent roadshow ...

item.title: 56-year-old man faces court after making repeated nuisance calls to emergency services
item.description: A 56-year-old man from Chifley has been arrested and charged with 10 counts of making vexatious calls to emergency services after allegedly harassing operators multiple times. Police confirmed the man...

item.title: Bangkok music bar fire kills 27, injures dozens
item.description: A devastating fire broke out at a music bar in Bangkok, resulting in at least 27 fatalities and over 50 injuries. The incident, which occurred in a crowded establishment, has sparked concerns about fi...

item.title: Philippines Strengthens Legal Efforts to Enforce 2016 South China Sea Ruling
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formed a dedicated maritime unit to sustain the gains from the 2016 South China Sea arbitral ruling, which invalidated China's 'nine-dash line' claim. Justice Secre...

item.title: ADC Denies Fake INEC Portal Upload, Calls for Investigation into Alleged Forgery
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has categorically denied claims that a group uploaded fake names to the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) portal, calling the allegation a delibera...

item.title: David Beckham jokes that Victoria was 'celebrating on the inside' after her muted reaction to England's World Cup goal goes viral
item.description: David Beckham has responded light-heartedly after his wife, Victoria Beckham, became the subject of widespread social media attention during England's World Cup quarter-final victory over Norway in Mi...

item.title: Ebola outbreak in DRC spreads to two new provinces, surpassing 700 deaths
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has expanded to two additional northeastern provinces, Haut-Uele and Tshopo, according to the country's public health institute. The co...

item.title: Tash Reflects on Raising Betty Bayo's Children Amid Custody Dispute
item.description: Tash, known as Hiram Gitau, has shared his confidence that the late Betty Bayo would be proud of how he's raising their children, Sky Victor and Danny. Speaking during a church service, he asked the c...

item.title: President Marcos Recognizes Filipina Tennis Star Alex Eala with Presidential Citation
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. recently conferred a presidential citation on Filipina tennis star Alex Eala, acknowledging her outstanding achievements in the sport. The honor comes amid Eala's rising...

item.title: Federal Government Urges Alberta Residents to Remain in Canada Ahead of Referendum
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney's attendance at the Calgary Stampede highlighted efforts by the federal government and pro-Canada advocates to persuade Albertans to remain in Canada as the province prepare...

item.title: Supreme Court halts Tamil Nadu's cow slaughter ban order
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has stayed a directive from the Madras High Court that imposed a blanket ban on cow and calf slaughter across Tamil Nadu, including on days like Bakrid. The interim order wa...

item.title: Gov Suspends Exam Fee Hike After Public Pressure, Obi Praises Decision
item.description: Presidential candidate Peter Obi has praised the federal government for suspending plans to increase registration fees for upcoming exams like WASSCE and NECO. The decision, announced by the Ministry ...

item.title: TNA Wrestling Star KC Navarro Returns After Knee Injury, Prepares for Upcoming Lockdown Event
item.description: KC Navarro, a rising star in TNA Wrestling, has made a remarkable recovery from a torn ACL injury that kept him out of action for several months. In an interview with Fox News Digital, Navarro reveale...

item.title: Extradition Hearing for UK Man Accused of Triple Murder Begins in South Africa
item.description: A British citizen of Zimbabwean heritage, Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, 45, has appeared in court in Johannesburg, South Africa, following an extradition request from UK authorities. He is accused of murd...

item.title: JD Vance Faces Backlash from GOP Donors Over Israel Criticisms
item.description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance is facing growing backlash from influential Republican donors and pro-Israel activists due to his critical stance on Israel and Iran. According to a Politico report, Vance...

item.title: DJ Yousuke Yukimatsu's Journey Through Music and Peace After Battling Cancer
item.description: Japanese DJ Yousuke Yukimatsu, known as 'your favourite DJ’s favourite DJ,' has transformed his life after surviving a brain tumour. His journey began with a near-fatal epileptic seizure in 2024, whic...

item.title: PM Shehbaz meets Qatari leader Sheikh Tamim amid mourning for ex-emir Sheikh Hamad
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif met Qatar’s Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani in Doha, extending condolences on the death of Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, Qatar’s former emir who passed away at ...

item.title: South Africa Freezes Municipal Funding to Combat Financial Mismanagement
item.description: The National Treasury of South Africa has temporarily frozen R13.5 billion ($825.8 million) in funding for 69 municipalities across all nine provinces, targeting financial mismanagement ahead of the N...

item.title: U.S.-Iran conflict escalates: Missile strikes, oil prices spike as Gulf nations brace for retaliation
item.description: The situation in the Gulf has intensified following a series of coordinated attacks and counterattacks between Iran and the United States. Iran's Revolutionary Guards launched missile strikes on U.S. ...
